name: Lokapalas
description: The Lokapalas, also known as the Guardians of the Directions, are the deities who rule the specific directions of space according to Hindu cosmology. They are powerful protectors and upholders of dharma, ensuring cosmic order and balance. Traditionally, there are eight Lokapalas, each associated with a cardinal or ordinal direction. These are Indra (East), Agni (Southeast), Yama (South), Surya (Southwest), Varuna (West), Vayu (Northwest), Kubera (North), and Ishana (Northeast). They are often depicted with specific attributes, mounts, and weapons that symbolize their power and responsibilities. Indra, the king of the gods, wields the Vajra (thunderbolt) and rides Airavata, the white elephant. Agni, the god of fire, rides a ram and carries a flaming spear. Yama, the god of death, rides a buffalo and carries a mace or a noose. Surya, the sun god, rides a chariot drawn by horses. Varuna, the god of water, rides a Makara (sea creature) and carries a noose. Vayu, the god of wind, rides a deer and carries a banner. Kubera, the god of wealth, rides a man and carries a mace or a treasure pot. Ishana, a form of Shiva, rides a bull and carries a trident. The Lokapalas are revered for their strength, wisdom, and their role in maintaining the stability of the universe. They are invoked for protection, prosperity, and guidance in various rituals and prayers. Their presence is felt in temples and sacred spaces, where they are often depicted as guardians of the entrances and boundaries, warding off evil and ensuring the sanctity of the area. Their collective power ensures the righteous conduct of mortals and gods alike, thereby upholding the cosmic dharma.
subtitle: Guardians of the Directions

keyMyths.title: Indra's Battle with Vritra
keyMyths.description: Indra, as the Lokapala of the East, battles Vritra, a serpent demon who withholds the waters of the world, symbolizing the triumph of good over evil and the restoration of cosmic order. Indra's victory ensures rainfall and prosperity for the world.

keyMyths.title: Yama's Judgment
keyMyths.description: Yama, the Lokapala of the South, judges the souls of the deceased, deciding their fate based on their actions in life. His role emphasizes the importance of righteousness and the consequences of karma.

keyMyths.title: Kubera's Wealth
keyMyths.description: Kubera, the Lokapala of the North, is the treasurer of the gods and the keeper of wealth. He bestows prosperity and abundance upon those who are righteous and deserving, but also punishes greed and avarice.

sources.author: Roshen Dalal
